sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.3 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$G=A _{H}B$be the generalized free product of groups$A$and$B$with amalgamated subgroup$H$. Is$(G)=1$in the following cases?$H$is finite cyclic and$H_G=1$.$H$is finite cyclic and either$(A) H_G=1$or$(B) H_G=1$.$H_G=1$and$H$satisfies the minimum condition on subgroups.$(G) H=1$.$A$and$B$are free groups,$H$is finitely generated and at least one of$|A:H|$or$|B:H|$is infinite.$H$is infinite cyclic a...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
